question: What should I bring to a giveaway event?
answer: All Teens must show a valid high school ID, or any other proof of High School enrollment at check-in, to be admitted. We will also accept a photo or screenshot of your school ID if you do not have a physical card.

question: Who can shop with you?
answer: Unfortunately, due to maximum space capacity, only students who have a scheduled appointment will be admitted into the dress area.  We will have volunteers available to assist you.

question: How long will the appointment be?
answer: To ensure every teen has a great experience, shopping is limited to your 1½-hour appointment window after check-in.Please arrive on time, bring your school ID or proof of enrollment, and make sure your waiver is submitted so you can use your full time to find the perfect dress and accessory.If you are under 18, a parent or guardian must submit a waiver before arrival or you will not be able to shop.We must close promptly after the final appointment of the day.With thousands of dresses in sizes 0–30, we’re confident you’ll find something amazing!

question: How will dress try-ons work?
answer: Since a lot of students will be shopping at the same time and we have a limited number of dressing rooms, you may need to share a dressing room, and we may limit how many dresses you can take in at once so everyone gets a chance to try things on.To make changing easier, we recommend wearing clothes that are easy to change in and out of, like slip-on shoes and athletic wear or a swimsuit underneath. Please also limit personal items like backpacks or large purses since we don’t have a place to store them.Please avoid strong perfume, jewelry that could snag dresses, and heavy makeup that might stain them.Also, no food or drinks (except water) are allowed in the dress area to help keep the dresses in great condition.

question: What about parking and transportation?
answer: Parking is free but can be very limited, especially during weekends. Parking is also available behind the building, with access to a breezeway that leads to the front of the building.  To plan your journey via public transportation, please visit www.VTA.Org to plan your trip.

question: What if I need to cancel or reschedule an appointment?
answer: We understand how last-minute issues can arise. If you know you are unable to make your appointment, please be courteous and cancel your appointment as soon as possible so we can provide an opportunity for other students to schedule their preferred appointment date. Rescheduling is easy!  Just make sure you ‘cancel’ your original appointment before rescheduling.

question: Can I take pics?
answer: We have a beautiful photo backdrop where you and your friends can strike a pose and take as many photos as you like! We DO NOT allow any photos while inside the dressing rooms when other students are changing.

question: Do you allow returns or exchanges?
answer: We want everyone to SPARKLE and SHINE for their event! However, we do not allow returns or exchanges.

question: Can I book multiple appointments?
answer: To provide other students with the opportunity to shop at our boutique, once you attend and receive a dress, you may not sign up to return on another date. Multiple appointments will be automatically deleted.

question: How do group appointments work?
answer: Group reservations must have a minimum of 15 students and must be able to book during the weekday.  For more information and requests, email Rebecca Flores at: Rebecca@princessproject.org
